title: two-trait split leaks into every call site as two positional args
description: JmapChatClient::new(transport, auth, base_url) requires every call site to pass two separate arguments (transport, auth) in a fixed positional order that has no obvious ergonomic grouping — callers who want 'just the defaults' still write JmapChatClient::new(DefaultTransport, NoneAuth, url) rather than JmapChatClient::new(url). The split is correct conceptually (transport \!= credentials) but the positional API does not surface that grouping: a reader sees two opaque arguments before the URL and must know their meaning. Three alternatives: (a) introduce a ClientConfig struct with transport and auth fields and a Default implementation so the common case becomes JmapChatClient::new(ClientConfig::default(), url); (b) provide a convenience constructor new_plain(auth, url) that hard-codes DefaultTransport and omits transport from the common-case call site entirely; (c) accept a builder JmapChatClient::builder().transport(t).auth(a).base_url(url).build(). Any of these reduces the cognitive cost of the common case without removing the full-control path.
close_reason: Added new_plain(auth, url) convenience constructor to JmapChatClient; DefaultTransport hidden from common-case call sites
